title: 55M with Exertional Chest Pain
summary: A 55-year-old male presents with worsening substernal chest pressure upon exertion.
chief_complaint: Chest pain when walking up stairs.
hpi: Patient reports a 3-week history of episodic substernal chest pressure radiating to his left jaw. The pain occurs after walking roughly two flights of stairs and is relieved by 5 minutes of rest. He denies palpitations, diaphoresis, or syncope. He had a similar episode 6 months ago that he ignored.
family_history: Father died of myocardial infarction at age 60

general: No fever, chills, or weight loss.
respiratory: Negative for shortness of breath or cough.

physical_exam.finding: RRR, normal S1/S2. No murmurs, rubs, or gallops. No JVD.
CTA bilaterally, no wheezes or crackles.
No lower extremity edema, 2+ pulses bilaterally.

labs.name: LDL Cholesterol
labs.interpretation: Elevated LDL, indicating sub-optimal lipid control despite statin therapy.

labs.name: Hemoglobin A1c
labs.interpretation: Elevated A1c, consistent with diagnosis of Type 2 Diabetes.

imaging.summary: Normal sinus rhythm, no acute ST/T wave changes, no Q waves.
imaging.interpretation: No evidence of acute ischemia or prior myocardial infarction on resting ECG.
Unremarkable study, no evidence of heart failure.

red_flags: Significant family history of premature CAD

teaching_points.explanation: Stable angina pectoris typically involves predictable, exertional chest pain relieved by rest or nitroglycerin.
Requires an ECG to rule out Acute Coronary Syndrome (ACS) and serial cardiac biomarkers.
Paramount for long-term survival, especially smoking cessation and tightening glycemic and lipid control.

model_differential.rationale: Classic exertional chest pressure radiating to jaw, relieved by rest, in a patient with multiple risk factors.
Must be ruled out given the worsening nature of the pain and cardiac risk factors, though currently stable at rest.
Can cause substernal chest discomfort, but less likely to be predictably exertional and relieved by rest.

model_soap.subjective: 55yo male presents with 3 weeks of exertional substernal chest pain radiating to left jaw, relieved by rest. PMH significant for HTN, HLD, T2DM. Social history includes 30 pack-year smoking.
model_soap.objective: BP 145/90, HR 82. CV exam normal, no murmurs. Lungs clear. ECG normal sinus rhythm with no acute ischemic changes. Troponin negative.
model_soap.assessment: Stable angina pectoris with multiple uncontrolled cardiac risk factors.
model_soap.plan: Initiate Aspirin 81mg, maximize statin therapy, prescribe PRN sublingual nitroglycerin, refer for outpatient stress testing, counseling on smoking cessation.

model_assessment_plan.plan: Presentation is highly suspicious for CAD. Start ASA 81mg daily and PRN NTG. Order outpatient exercise stress echocardiogram.
BP elevated today. Continue Lisinopril, monitor.
LDL elevated. Increase Atorvastatin to 40mg daily.
